title: epipogium kentingense
intro: Epipogium kentingense, also known as the Kenting epipogium, is a rare and unique species of orchid native to Taiwan. It is a type of epipogium, a subfamily of orchids that obtain their nutrients by parasitizing fungi associated with the roots of trees, rather than through photosynthesis. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Traditional herbal preparations of Epipogium kentingense include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat conditions such as arthritis, cardiovascular disease, and digestive disorders.
